---
title: "Your Document Title"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2023-02-22"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c("rmarkdown", "tidyverse", "fs", "stringr", "here", "utils", "logger")
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "test_unmatched.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

```{r}
data <- import(here("data", "processed", "merged_edm_data.RData"), trust = TRUE)
data_summary <- import(here("data", "processed", "merged_edm_data_with_summary.RData"), trust = TRUE)
annual_data <- import(here("data","processed", "annual_return_edm.rds"),
                      trust = TRUE) 
```


```{r}
data_summary$summary
data_summary$data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water",
         year == 2022,
         merge_quality == "no_match") %>% 
  pull(site_name_ea) %>% unique()
```


### Fixing South West Water 2022 and 2023

```{r}
individ_data <- import(here("data","processed", "combined_edm_data.RData"),
                       trust = TRUE) 

# 2022 is problematic
leftsouth22 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2022) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

rightsouth22 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2022) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

left_join(leftsouth22, rightsouth22) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))

# 2023
leftsouth23 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2023) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.)))) %>% 
  select(-contains("site"))

rightsouth23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)
rightsouth23$u

# this fixes 2023
left_join(leftsouth23, rightsouth23) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))
```



### Fixing Yorkshire Water 2022

```{r}
individ_data <- import(here("data","processed", "combined_edm_data.RData"),
                       trust = TRUE) 

york22 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water", year == 2022) %>% 
  mutate(unique_id = paste0("YW", unique_id))

york23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)

left_join(york22, york23, by = join_by(water_company, unique_id)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr)) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)
```


### Fixing Thames Water 2023

```{r}
leftthames23 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Thames Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))  %>% 
  mutate(across(
    where(is.character) & matches("site"), tolower))

rightthames23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Thames Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))  %>% 
  mutate(across(
    where(is.character) & matches("site"), tolower))

left_join(leftthames23, rightthames23,
          by = join_by(year, site_name_wa_sc, unique_id),
          suffix = c("", "_annual")) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))
  
rightthames23 %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.character), tolower))
```


### Fixing merge key

```{r}
data <- data_summary$data
dat <- load_data()

water_companies <- unique(data$water_company)

for (i in seq_along(water_companies)) {
  company <- water_companies[i]
  print(company)
  
  join <- data %>% 
  filter(water_company == company, year == 2023) %>% 
  pull(key_join) %>% 
  unique()
  print(join)

}



current_company <- "Southern Water"
current_year <- 2023
      
# Get individual and annual spill data for current company-year
# Remove columns that are all NA as they may differ between datasets
indiv <- dat$individual %>%
filter(water_company == current_company, year == current_year) %>%
select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

annual <- annual_data %>%
  filter(water_company == current_company, 
         year == current_year, 
         !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

# Define joining keys: common columns plus location identifier
# In 2023, we have unique id, so we use that as the join key
cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")
key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
if (current_year == 2023 & "unique_id" %in% key_join) {
  key_join <- c("water_company", "year", "unique_id")
}
key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)




data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Southern Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  pull(key_join) %>% 
  unique()

data %>% 
  filter(year == 2023) 

unique(data$key_join)
grepl("unique_id", unique(data$key_join))
unique(data$key_join)[grepl("unique_id", unique(data$key_join))]

x <- unique(data$key_join)[[3]]
x <- unique(data$key_join)[[1]]

x
if ("unique_id" %in% x) {
  y = x[x!="unique_id"]
} else {
  y = x
}
y

subset(x, x!="unique_id")

x[x!="unique_id"]
```
